features: F12: App Shell & Routing — React Router setup with '/' and '/c/:conversationId' routes, two-column layout (260px sidebar + flex chat area), global CSS variables, Inter font loaded

F13: Sidebar — Conversation List — fetches and displays conversations sorted by recency, title + relative timestamp + truncated preview per item, active state with blue left-border, skeleton loaders, delete with confirmation, pinned 'New Chat' button

F14: Chat Area & Message Rendering — loads messages for active conversation, right-aligned blue user bubbles, left-aligned dark assistant bubbles with Markdown + syntax highlighting (oneDark), skeleton loaders, auto-scroll with user-scroll-pause detection

F15: Chat Input — auto-expanding textarea (up to ~6 lines), blue animated focus glow, send on Enter / Shift+Enter for newline, disabled state during streaming, fixed bottom position with gradient fade

criteria.name: two_column_layout_renders
criteria.description: On viewports ≥ 768px the app renders a fixed 260px sidebar and a flex-fill chat area side by side with no horizontal overflow; sidebar background is #111827 and root background is #0a0a0f. On viewports < 768px the sidebar is either hidden/collapsed or stacked above the chat area — the layout must not overflow or clip content at 375px viewport width.

criteria.name: routing_new_chat_redirect
criteria.description: Navigating to '/' either redirects to a new conversation route '/c/:id' or renders a valid empty-chat landing state without a blank white screen or JS console error.

criteria.name: conversation_route_loads_messages
criteria.description: Navigating directly to '/c/:conversationId' fetches and renders the correct message history for that conversation within 2 seconds on a local network. An invalid or non-existent ID renders a visible error or empty state (e.g. 'Conversation not found') rather than crashing or showing a blank screen.

criteria.name: sidebar_conversation_list_populated
criteria.description: The sidebar displays all conversations returned by GET /api/conversations, sorted newest-first, each showing: the conversation title, a relative timestamp (e.g. '2 hours ago'), and a truncated preview string of ≤ 80 characters. When GET /api/conversations returns an empty array, a visible empty state (e.g. 'No conversations yet') is shown instead of a blank sidebar.

criteria.name: sidebar_skeleton_loaders
criteria.description: While the conversations fetch is in-flight, at least 3 animated shimmer skeleton rows are visible in the sidebar in place of conversation items; no raw loading spinner is used.

criteria.name: sidebar_active_state
criteria.description: The currently active conversation item has a visible 3px left blue border (#3b82f6) and a lighter background compared to inactive items; switching conversations updates this highlight immediately.

criteria.name: sidebar_delete_with_confirmation
criteria.description: Hovering a conversation item reveals a delete button; clicking it triggers a confirmation step (dialog or inline confirm) before calling DELETE /api/conversations/{id}. On success the item is removed from the list without a full page reload. If the DELETE call fails (non-2xx response), an error message is shown to the user and the item remains in the list.

criteria.name: new_chat_button_creates_conversation
criteria.description: Clicking the 'New Chat' button (full-width, blue background, pinned to sidebar top) calls POST /api/conversations, then navigates to '/c/:newId' and renders an empty chat state. If the POST call fails (non-2xx or network error), an error message is surfaced to the user and the route does not change.

criteria.name: message_bubble_visual_distinction
criteria.description: User messages render right-aligned with background #2563eb and white text; assistant messages render left-aligned with background #1e293b and white text; both use rounded-2xl corners; max-width is ≤ 70% for user and ≤ 80% for assistant.

criteria.name: message_area_skeleton_loaders
criteria.description: While the messages fetch for a conversation is in-flight, at least 3 animated shimmer skeleton rows are visible in the chat area (alternating left/right alignment to mimic the bubble layout); no raw loading spinner is used. Once the fetch resolves the skeletons are replaced by the actual message list.

criteria.name: assistant_markdown_rendering
criteria.description: Assistant messages containing Markdown (bold, italic, bullet lists, numbered lists, inline code) render the formatted output via react-markdown + remark-gfm rather than displaying raw Markdown syntax characters (e.g. '**bold**' must render as bold text, not the asterisks).

criteria.name: code_block_syntax_highlighting
criteria.description: Assistant messages containing fenced code blocks with a language identifier (e.g. ```javascript) render with react-syntax-highlighter using the oneDark theme, a dark background (#0d1117), a language label in the top-right corner, and a copy-to-clipboard button. Fenced code blocks with no language identifier still render with the dark background and copy button, with the language label omitted or shown as 'plaintext'.

criteria.name: chat_area_auto_scroll
criteria.description: When new messages are appended to the conversation, the chat area automatically scrolls to the bottom. If the user has manually scrolled up more than 100px from the bottom, auto-scroll is suppressed until the user scrolls back within 100px of the bottom.

criteria.name: chat_input_auto_expand
criteria.description: The chat textarea starts at 1 line height and grows with content up to a maximum of ~6 lines (~144px), then becomes internally scrollable; after a message is sent the textarea resets to 1 line.

criteria.name: chat_input_focus_glow
criteria.description: Clicking into the chat textarea applies a visible blue box-shadow glow (matching 'box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #3b82f6') on the textarea element; the border/glow is absent when the textarea is not focused.

criteria.name: chat_input_gradient_fade
criteria.description: The chat input bar is fixed to the bottom of the chat area and is visually separated from the message list by a gradient fade (e.g. a pseudo-element or wrapper div with a linear-gradient from transparent to the chat background color). The gradient must be visible when there are enough messages to scroll and must not obscure the last message bubble when scrolled to the bottom.

criteria.name: send_on_enter_shift_newline
criteria.description: Pressing Enter in the chat textarea submits the message (equivalent to clicking the send button); pressing Shift+Enter inserts a newline character without submitting. Pressing Enter on an empty or whitespace-only textarea does not submit and does not call any API.

criteria.name: input_disabled_during_streaming
criteria.description: While an assistant response is being streamed, the chat textarea and send button are both visibly disabled (disabled attribute set, reduced opacity or cursor:not-allowed applied). Pressing Enter or clicking the send button during streaming has no effect and does not enqueue a second API call. Once streaming completes the input returns to an enabled, editable state.

criteria.name: inter_font_and_color_palette_applied
criteria.description: The computed font-family on body text resolves to 'Inter' (or the system fallback stack); text primary color (#f1f5f9) is applied to message content and text secondary (#94a3b8) is applied to timestamps and labels.
